note: Use analytical_depth, evidence_sensitivity, and source_credulity in lieu of any 'intelligence' axis. These axes describe stable cognitive and behavioral tendencies that modulate how cohorts and heroes process information and decide to act.

items.label: Stubbornness
items.definition: Tendency to resist updating prior beliefs even when presented with new evidence or persuasive argument.
items.low_anchor: Updates beliefs readily on new information; revises priors with light pressure.
items.high_anchor: Holds prior beliefs even against strong contradictory evidence; doubles down under challenge.

items.label: Openness to Change
items.definition: Willingness to adopt new identities, narratives, group memberships, or behavioral repertoires.
items.low_anchor: Prefers familiar frames and routines; suspicious of novelty.
items.high_anchor: Actively seeks new framings, communities, and ways of acting.

items.label: Evidence Sensitivity
items.definition: Degree to which belief updates are weighted by the quality, specificity, and verifiability of evidence presented.
items.low_anchor: Treats all claims roughly equally regardless of evidence quality.
items.high_anchor: Weights well-sourced, falsifiable, replicated evidence much more heavily than anecdote.

items.label: Source Credulity
items.definition: Default tendency to accept claims based on the identity of the speaker rather than the substance.
items.low_anchor: Skeptical by default; demands corroboration regardless of speaker.
items.high_anchor: Accepts claims uncritically when made by familiar or in-group sources.

items.label: Authority Deference
items.definition: Tendency to defer to formal authorities (governments, employers, credentialed experts, religious leaders) on contested questions.
items.low_anchor: Treats authority claims with the same scrutiny as any other claim.
items.high_anchor: Defers to recognized authorities even when their reasoning is unstated or weak.

items.label: Contrarianism
items.definition: Tendency to take positions opposed to perceived majority or mainstream consensus regardless of substance.
items.low_anchor: Comfortable agreeing with consensus when it matches one's reasoning.
items.high_anchor: Reflexively opposes whatever is currently dominant; identity tied to dissent.

items.label: Sycophancy
items.definition: Tendency to align stated views with whoever one is currently talking to or with high-status in-group voices.
items.low_anchor: States views consistently regardless of audience.
items.high_anchor: Mirrors the views of the present audience or dominant in-group voice.

items.label: Risk Tolerance
items.definition: Willingness to accept personal cost (legal, financial, reputational, physical) for uncertain reward.
items.low_anchor: Strongly avoids any personal exposure to downside.
items.high_anchor: Comfortable accepting significant downside for plausible upside.

items.label: Coordination Capacity
items.definition: Capability to organize joint action with others, including planning, communication, and role assignment.
items.low_anchor: Struggles to coordinate beyond informal one-to-one contact.
items.high_anchor: Routinely coordinates large group actions with clear roles and timing.

items.label: Mobilization Capacity
items.definition: Capability to convert latent sympathy into concrete public action by self or others (turnout, attendance, contribution).
items.low_anchor: Rarely converts interest into showing up or contributing.
items.high_anchor: Reliably converts diffuse sentiment into visible action by self and recruits.

items.label: Attention Decay Rate
items.definition: Speed at which an issue fades from active attention in the absence of fresh stimulus.
items.low_anchor: Sustains attention to an issue for many ticks without reinforcement.
items.high_anchor: Drops issues quickly; needs constant fresh stimulus to stay engaged.

items.label: Media Susceptibility
items.definition: Degree to which exposure to media framing shifts attention, salience, and emotional state.
items.low_anchor: Media exposure has little effect on internal state.
items.high_anchor: Strongly entrained by whatever media the cohort consumes.

items.label: Spiral-of-Silence Susceptibility
items.definition: Degree to which perceived minority status suppresses public expression of privately held views.
items.low_anchor: Speaks publicly regardless of perceived majority alignment.
items.high_anchor: Goes silent in public the moment the perceived majority appears to disagree.

items.label: Identity Salience Sensitivity
items.definition: Responsiveness of behavior to cues that activate group identity (symbols, threats, in-group framing).
items.low_anchor: Identity cues do not noticeably shift behavior.
items.high_anchor: Strongly shifts stance, expression, and trust when identity is activated.

items.label: Analytical Depth
items.definition: Tendency to decompose claims into structure, mechanism, and assumptions before responding.
items.low_anchor: Reacts to surface frame and headline; skips structural analysis.
items.high_anchor: Routinely models mechanisms, distinguishes claims, and surfaces hidden premises.
